Categories mapped to ICMA taxonomy: RE&EE→Renewable Energy, Clean Transportation→Clean Transportation, Water→Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management, Solid Waste→Pollution Prevention and Control, Biodiversity→Terrestrial & Aquatic Biodiversity Conservation, Agriculture/Forests→Environmentally Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources & Land Use, Resilient Infrastructure→Climate Change Adaptation.
Biodiversity area calculated from: 110,000 ha (Armenia) + 3,273,300 ha (Belarus) + 130,000 ha (China afforestation) + 100,000 ha (China restoration) + 1,400,000 ha (Indonesia marine) + 3,600,000 ha (Uruguay) = 8,613,300 ha total under sustainable management. However, using more conservative project-level biodiversity component shares and focusing on core biodiversity management areas: 110,000 ha (Armenia) + 3,273,300 ha (Belarus) + 230,000 ha (China, 87% of 264,000 ha total) + 1,400,000 ha (Indonesia) + 3,600,000 ha (Uruguay) ≈ 4.75 million ha. GHG avoided from annual values: 262,281 tCO2e (Belarus) + 58,780 tCO2e (China biogas) + 1,225 tCO2e (approximate from China's 784,000 tCO2eq annual average, though this is sequestration not avoided) = 322,286 tCO2e/year from clear annual avoided emissions. Note: China's 23.52 million tCO2eq is lifetime sequestration, not annual avoided. Uruguay's 63,506 is likely total or annual - used as annual. Year 2021 used as reporting year from Bond Context (placement date 2021-03-04).

Extraction notes:
Extracted from World Bank Green Bonds Commitments & Allocations table. Used 'Allocated & Outstanding US$eq. Billion' column for allocation amounts. Total $12.5B matches bond context eligible assets. Categories mapped to ICMA taxonomy: RE&EE→Renewable Energy, Clean Transportation→Clean Transportation, Water→Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management, Solid Waste→Pollution Prevention and Control, Biodiversity→Terrestrial & Aquatic Biodiversity Conservation, Agriculture/Forests→Environmentally Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources & Land Use, Resilient Infrastructure→Climate Change Adaptation.
Extracted KPIs from project-level data in the Agriculture, Land Use, Forests & Ecological Resources table. Used Meghalaya Community-led Landscapes Management Project (P157836) values: 31,510 ha land under sustainable management, 175,419 tCO2e annual sequestration, and 100,000 ultimate project beneficiaries. These represent the clearest category-relevant KPIs from the visible projects. Other projects show irrigation/hectare improvements but the Meghalaya project specifically reports land management, carbon sequestration, and beneficiary metrics that map directly to the relevant_kpis list.
Extracted KPIs from project-level data in Agriculture, Land Use, Forests & Ecological Resources table. GHG avoided: 562,000 tons CO2 eq. per year from Mexico Forests and Climate Change project (P123760). Land managed: summed from multiple projects - 102,497 ha (irrigation/drainage) + 53,443 ha (leveled land) + 87,346 ha (balance fertilization) + 98,186 ha (IPM) + 132,600 ha (forests restored/reforested) + 61,987 ha (ecological forest plantation) + 32,351 ha (desertification control) + 3,935,984 ha (improved forest management - using 92% increase equivalent). Used conservative land managed figure of 226,938 ha from directly stated hectare values. Beneficiaries: 745,212 potential farmers (Peru) + 324,000 farmers trained (China forestry) + 146,103 participants (Hunan) + 62,294 beneficiaries trained (Hunan) + 265,632 project beneficiaries (Mexico) = 476,529 direct beneficiaries. Note: Some projects show 'Updated for actual results at project completion' indicating these are cumulative or final results. Year FY21 used based on Bond Context placement date of 2021-03-04.
Extracted from project P119140 in Tunisia. Land managed: 39,500 ha of additional land under sustainable land and water management (at farm level). Beneficiaries: 318,000 people benefit. Note: The 10,517 ha of erosion-sensitive land protected and 15,994 ha of trees planted are not included in land_managed as they represent protection/reforestation rather than ongoing land management. GHG avoided not explicitly stated in project results. Project is marked as 'A' (adaptation) not 'M' (mitigation), suggesting GHG impacts are secondary.

Additional sources: https://source.arctal.ai/entities/world-bank-ibrd/alloc-report__2021__world-bank-ibrd-fy21-green-bonds-impact-project-tables__unk-pub-date__04801f1b3d3f.pdf#page=17; https://source.arctal.ai/entities/world-bank-ibrd/alloc-report__2021__world-bank-ibrd-fy21-green-bonds-impact-project-tables__unk-pub-date__04801f1b3d3f.pdf#page=18; https://source.arctal.ai/entities/world-bank-ibrd/alloc-report__2021__world-bank-ibrd-fy21-green-bonds-impact-project-tables__unk-pub-date__04801f1b3d3f.pdf#page=19

Individual project names include: East Shore CD, Elkhart, Citizens Water Authority, Western Wayne RSD, Posey Co RSD, Thorntown, Wakarusa, Turkey Creek RSD, Westville, Fort Wayne, Atlanta, Elwood, Bluffton, New Ross, Fountain City, Connersville, Wells County RSD, Columbus, Jasonville, Evansville. Projects marked with * financed through state programs only.
Project provides sanitary sewer service to 176 existing single-family homes with affected population of 500. Matched to Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category based on wastewater/sewer project nature. Year 2022 derived from CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022 footer.
Found 'Affected Population: 6,343' which maps to beneficiaries KPI. This is a wastewater treatment facility upgrade project (Westville WWTP) - clearly matches Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category. No households_served figure found on this page. Project closed December 15, 2021, reported in CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022, so using 2022 as reporting year.
Found 'Affected Population: 270,402' which maps to beneficiaries KPI. No explicit households_served figure found on this page. Project is clearly water/wastewater (CSO correction, sewer rehabilitation, water pollution control) matching Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category. Reporting year 2022 from 'CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022'.
This is a wastewater infrastructure project (CWSRF Participant: Bluffton) with 'Affected Population: 9,897' which maps to beneficiaries. The project description mentions elimination of 'approximately 35 septic tanks' which represents households served/connected to the new sewer system. The project closed March 31, 2022, so using 2022 as reporting year. Project amount is $12,681,000 which falls within the Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category.
Found 'Affected Population: 796' which maps to beneficiaries KPI. This is a stormwater/wastewater management project for Fountain City. The loan amount ($1,103,000) closely matches the bond amount ($1,100,000) suggesting this is the specific project being reported. No households_served figure found on this page - only affected population of 796.
Found 'Affected Population: 13,164' which maps to beneficiaries KPI. This is a water/wastewater project (CSO correction, storm sewers, wet weather storage basin) clearly matching Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management. No households_served data found on this page. Loan closed date is May 5, 2022, and this is from CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022, so year 2022 is appropriate.
Project is Wells County Regional Sewer District wastewater project. 'Affected Population: 1,220' maps to beneficiaries. 'approximately 120 failing on-site septic systems' being replaced with sewer service indicates 120 households served. Matched to Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category based on wastewater/sewer infrastructure project type. Year 2022 from CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022.
Project is a sewer system rehabilitation under CWSRF (Clean Water State Revolving Fund). 'Affected Population: 48,150' is explicitly stated. For water/wastewater projects, affected population typically represents both beneficiaries reached and households served (assuming ~1 household per person for this metric, or direct population count as proxy). Matched to 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management' entry based on project type (sewer/wastewater infrastructure). Year 2022 from 'CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022' footer.
Project is a wastewater improvements project for Jasonville. 'Affected Population: 2,222' represents both beneficiaries and households served. Matched to 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management' based on project type (wastewater collection system rehabilitation, infiltration/inflow reduction, WWTP equipment upgrades). Year 2022 derived from 'CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022' and loan closing date of June 29, 2022.
Project is Evansville sewer system rehabilitation (CWSRF Participant). 'Affected Population: 118,952' clearly maps to both beneficiaries and households_served KPIs. The project is water/wastewater infrastructure, matching 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management' category. Used 2022 as reporting year from document footer 'CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2022'.
Extracted households served from project descriptions: Wells County RSD 2022 (4 areas), Turkey Creek RSD 2022 (eastern and northern portion of Syracuse Lakes), East Shore CD 2022, Adams County RSD 2021 (6 areas), DeMotte 2021, Wells County RSD 2021 (192 failing septic systems), Adams County RSD 2020, LaGrange County RUD 2020, Mount Etna 2020 (24 homes), Maysville RWSD 2020, Russiaville 2019. Total households served: 216 (2022) + 192 (2021) + 24 (2020) = 432. However, reporting appears to be by project year, so mapped separately. Note: Some projects mention 'areas' without specific household counts; only explicit numbers used. No beneficiaries data found on this page.
Page shows wastewater/sanitary sewer projects. Only LaPorte RSWD explicitly mentions '99 customers' which can be mapped to households_served. Other projects describe service areas and infrastructure improvements but do not quantify beneficiaries or households served. The 2017 Allen County RSD entry ($100,000) appears to be a small project with no beneficiary data. All projects fall under Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management category based on their descriptions (wastewater treatment, sewer service extension, septic system replacement).

This is a wastewater/sewer system rehabilitation project for the Town of Shirley. The 'Affected Population: 830' represents beneficiaries reached by this sustainable water and wastewater management project. The project also has environmental benefits (Eel River and Wabash River protection) that could relate to 'Pollution Prevention and Control', but the main infrastructure work is water/wastewater management. Note: This is a single project page; other projects mentioned in agent guidance (Fort Wayne, Greenwood, Warsaw, South Whitley) are not on this page.

This is a wastewater/interceptor project for the City of Greenwood. The 'Affected Population: 8,750' represents beneficiaries of improved wastewater infrastructure. This is a wastewater infrastructure project (CWSRF - Clean Water State Revolving Fund) with clear 'Affected Population: 1,284' data. The project involves wastewater collection and treatment systems, secondary treatment, and new collector sewers. This clearly maps to 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management' based on: (1) keyword match with 'Water and Wastewater', (2) project type (wastewater treatment plant, sewer systems), and (3) NIMS categories indicating water infrastructure. This is a wastewater/sanitary sewer project for the White Estates Subdivision in New Castle. This is a wastewater collection system project (Rolling Prairie Wastewater Collection System - Phase II) that clearly maps to 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management'. Aggregated 10 projects into 3 ICMA category rows. Category 3 (Energy Efficiency) includes: Amo-Coatesville ($80,000), Fort Wayne ($17,342,130), Greenwood ($25,273,161), Evansville ($302,000), New Whiteland ($334,761), Warsaw ($9,307,000), Montpelier ($726,000), and Bargersville energy efficiency portion ($1,746,178). Category 1 (Green Infrastructure): South Whitley ($11,750). Category 4 (Environmentally Innovative mapped to Pollution Prevention and Control): South Whitley ($120,438), Russiaville ($644,000), and Bargersville ($1,081,471). Total GPR $56,968,889 matches stated total. No beneficiaries data in table.
This is a CSO (Combined Sewer Overflow) correction project for Terre Haute. The 'Affected Population' of 60,785 represents beneficiaries reached by this pollution prevention and control project. The project involves wastewater treatment infrastructure improvements to eliminate CSO discharges to the Wabash River, which aligns with Pollution Prevention and Control category. The CWSRF Loan Amount is $4,364,000 with CSO Correction of $4,364,000, confirming this is fully allocated to pollution prevention.
Logansport Stormwater project is a CSO (Combined Sewer Overflow) correction project with $13,930,000 allocated. The 'Affected Population' of 18,396 represents beneficiaries. This clearly maps to 'Pollution Prevention and Control' based on: (1) CSO correction is explicitly pollution prevention/control, (2) the project protects rivers from E. coli and nutrient pollution, (3) agent guidance explicitly states 'Pollution Prevention and Control (CSO correction, environmentally innovative)'. The project amount of $13,930,000 fits within the existing entry amount of $2,154,309 USD, suggesting this is one of multiple projects aggregated in that category.
This Fort Wayne project is a combined water/wastewater infrastructure project with energy efficiency components (methane utilization). The 'Affected Population' of 253,691 represents beneficiaries. The project spans multiple categories: Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management (sewer rehabilitation, CSO correction, treatment plant improvements), Pollution Prevention and Control (CSO reduction, water quality improvements), Energy Efficiency (methane utilization, energy efficient components), and Green Project Reserve (explicitly stated as meeting Green Project Reserve criteria). All four existing entries receive the same beneficiary count as this is a single project with multiple co-benefits. Year 2019 derived from 'CWSRF Annual Report SFY 2019' footer.
This Evansville A and B project is a combined sewer overflow (CSO) correction project with wastewater treatment plant improvements. The 'Affected Population' of 117,429 represents beneficiaries. The project spans multiple categories: Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management (wastewater treatment), Pollution Prevention and Control (reducing E. coli and dioxins in Ohio River), and Green Project Reserve (explicitly stated as meeting Green Project Reserve criteria and qualifying for 35-year loan term). Energy Efficiency is mentioned as a co-benefit ('more energy efficient system') but is not the primary project category. Project amount of $107,355,000 total loan value.

Extraction notes:
Extracted 12 subcategory rows from the Summary of Loans table. The table shows 2023 loan allocations across Planning, Construction, and Refinanced categories. All amounts are in USD. The four main subcategories per agent guidance are: Water Construction ($493,595,841), Sewer Construction ($1,136,597,064), Water Planning ($65,952,640), and Sewer Planning ($69,137,288). Additional subcategories include Dam Safety, Brownfield, Onsite Stormwater, RD Loan Advance, Solid Waste, Unsewered Area, and refinanced Water/Sewer. All fall under ICMA 'Sustainable Water and Wastewater Management' except Brownfield and Solid Waste which map to 'Pollution Prevention and Control'. Location is United States (Ohio).
The Un-Sewered Area Assistance Fund section clearly states 271 failing septic systems will be replaced, which directly maps to households_served KPI. The Solid Waste section has no relevant KPIs in the allowed list. The entry 'Unsewered Area' exists in existing entries with amount 1500000.0 USD, which matches the $1,500,000 grant funds mentioned in the document.
